message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Mortality remains very high and unpredictable in CoViD-19, with intense public protection strategies tailored to preceived risk. Males are at greater risk of severe CoViD-19 complications. Genomic studies are in process to identify differences in host susceptibility to SARS-CoV-2 infection.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Genomic structures were examined for the
                    <jats:italic>ACE2</jats:italic>
                    gene that encodes angiotensin-converting enzyme 2, the obligate receptor for SARS-CoV-2. Variants in 213,158 exomes/genomes were integrated with ACE2 protein functional domains, and pathogenicity criteria from the American Society of Human Genetics and Genomics/Association for Molecular Pathology.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    483 variants were identified in the 19 exons of
                    <jats:italic>ACE2</jats:italic>
                    on the X chromosome. All variants were rare, including nine loss-of-function (potentially SARS-CoV-2 protective) alleles present only in female heterozygotes. Unopposed variant alleles were more common in males (262/3596 [7.3%] nucleotides) than females (9/3596 [0.25%] nucleotides,
                    <jats:italic>p</jats:italic>
                    <0.0001). 37 missense variants substituted amino acids in SARS-CoV-2 interacting regions or critical domains for transmembrane ACE2 expression. Four upstream open reading frames with 31 associated variants were identified. Excepting loss-of-function alleles, variants would not meet minimum criteria for classification as ‘Likely Pathogenic/beneficial’ if differential frequencies emerged in patients with CoViD-19.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Males are more exposed to consequences from a single variant
                    <jats:italic>ACE2</jats:italic>
                    allele. Common risk/beneficial alleles are unlikely in regions subject to evolutionary constraint.
                    <jats:italic>ACE2</jats:italic>
                    upstream open reading frames may have implications for aminoglycoside use in SARS-CoV-2-infected patients. For this SARS-CoV-2-interacting protein with pre-identified functional domains, pre-emptive functional and computational studies are encouraged to accelerate interpretations of genomic variation for personalised and public health use.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: COVID-19 genomic susceptibility: Definition of ACE2 variants relevant to human infection with SARS-CoV-2 in the context of ACMG/AMP Guidance
